blog_title: 5 Offbeat Destinations For A Spellbinding Kashmir Summer Tour
seo_title: 5 Offbeat Destinations For A Spellbinding Kashmir Summer Tour
description: Discover the ultimate travel packing list for every adventure. Pack smart, light, and stress-free with essential tips .
body_content:
Published by travelxploria on Mon Jun 16 2025

As soon as summer approaches, the one thing that most people think about apart from having ice creams and cold drinks is going on a nice, cool vacation. Are you planning to visit ‘Paradise on Earth’ this time? Great! Kashmir with its snow-capped peaks, vast beautiful meadows, and lovely lakes is indeed a sight to behold. However, this breathtaking place is much more than that!
Check out these 5 offbeat locations in Kashmir that you must explore to get the most out of your trip

Lamayuru- The Moon-land:

If you have ever dreamed about walking on the moon then you would definitely fall for Lamayuru. This particular area feels as if you are taking a walk on the moon due to its moon-like landform. Do you love to gather knowledge about new places? You will be delighted to know that Lamayuru has a very interesting history. Dated back to the 11th Century, a scholar named Mahasiddhacharya Naropa laid the foundation stone of the Lamayuru monastery. It’s believed that his prayers caused the drying up of a lake that took care of a whole village and as the water retreated, the place started to get moon-like craters. It takes just 3 hours to go to Lamayuru.

The unparalleled natural beauty of Chatpal:

While the striking scenery of Gulmarg, Yusmarg is quite well-known, the enchanting beauty of Kashmir’s Chatpal village is less heard of. It’s situated in the Anantnag district of Jammu and Kashmir. The rich green colors of mountains and lush forests create a scene of unmatched beauty. If this is a trip along with your family, then it’s the perfect place to spend some quality time with them over here, right in the midst of nature, away from the hustle and bustle of city life. You can also consider going hiking in the forests around Chatpal.

Daksum is fascinating:

Arriving at Daksum, you can see snow-capped mountains, secluded green meadows, and coniferous forests. In fact, every step in the Daksum Valley can be the ultimate reason for your joy in Kashmir. No matter what kind of person you are, you will find something or the other to like at Daksum— from the simplicity of rural life to the picturesque splendor of natural springs. It’s a great location for hiking and camping.

Watlab- Every naturalist’s dream:

You can enjoy the mind-boggling views of Wular Lake, the largest freshwater lake in Asia, from Watlab. Watlab is a famed site for bird lovers and naturalists that includes Himalayan Monals, Himalayan golden eagles, Barn Shallows, Rock Doves, and other rare birds. The shrine of Baba Shakur-Ud-Din is situated on a hilltop. You can indulge in different activities like Shikara rides, fishing, etc. This area is a beautiful blend of spirituality and natural beauty.

The picture of tranquility- Lolab Valley:

Wadi-e-Lolab or the Lolab Valley is one of the most peaceful and tranquil locations of Kashmir. Covered paddy fields, lovely springs, a lake, and apple orchards are just a few attractions that you shouldn’t miss. Brunai Valley, Potnai Valley, and Kalaroos Valley are the three mysterious valleys that make Lolab. If there is a hidden professional photographer in you then the scenic grandeur of Nagmarg meadows that separates Lolab from Bandipora district will be a dream picture to possess forever.
